description: W0.2 acceptance-fixture registry. Drives Issue #174 acceptance proof: a small user-pasteable COBOL program is transformed, compiled, executed, and behaviourally verified end-to-end. The HELLOW02 fixture is the canonical positive acceptance program (DISPLAY + WORKING-STORAGE + PERFORM VARYING + ADD); FILEIO-UNSUPPORTED proves the orchestrator blocks honestly with unsupported_cobol when source contains File I/O. Both fixtures are file-backed AND must work as paste-mode inputs through the BFF transform endpoint. Conforms to schemas/acceptance-fixture-v0.json.

fixtures.title: Hello W0.2
fixtures.description: Smallest user-pasteable W0.2 acceptance program. Exercises WORKING-STORAGE numeric fields, a bounded PERFORM VARYING loop, DISPLAY of literals concatenated with numeric variables, and ADD into an accumulator. Compiled by the build-test runner with cobc and executed with cobcrun; the generated Java is executed and its stdout compared against the cobol-runtime oracle.
fixtures.rationale: Proves the W0.2 acceptance criterion: a small, user-provided COBOL program flows through the agentic workflow end-to-end, the generated Java compiles and runs, and its output matches a real GnuCOBOL-derived oracle. Output bytes were reproduced from corpus/synthetic/programs/hello-w02.cbl with `cobc -x -free hello-w02.cbl && ./hello-w02`.

fixtures.title: File I/O is outside the W0.2 subset
fixtures.description: Negative acceptance fixture. The program declares FILE SECTION, FD, OPEN, READ, and CLOSE — all outside the W0.2 supported subset. The orchestrator MUST block the run with finalClassification 'blocked' and failureCode 'unsupported_cobol', surfacing the parser diagnostics in the evidence pack. The run MUST NOT produce Java output.

unsupportedConstructs.message: FILE SECTION is not supported in the W0.2 subset.
FD entries require File I/O, which is not supported in the W0.2 subset.
OPEN requires File I/O, which is not supported in the W0.2 subset.
READ requires File I/O, which is not supported in the W0.2 subset.
CLOSE requires File I/O, which is not supported in the W0.2 subset.

fixtures.rationale: Proves that programs outside the W0.2 supported subset are blocked honestly, with construct-level diagnostics, instead of producing misleading Java. Aligns with Issue #174 acceptance criterion: 'Unsupported programs are blocked honestly with construct-level explanation where possible.'
